passage.title: Bean Seed Light Test
passage.text: Our science club planted bean seeds in paper cups last month. Mina used a sunny window spot, but Joon used a dark shelf. After one week, Mina's plant looked taller because it got more light each day. The club measured each stem and wrote the numbers in a notebook. When Joon saw the difference, he changed to the sunny window. Two days later, his plant started to grow faster. The students talked about the test before choosing a better method. The group learned that a small change in place can help a plant grow better.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mostly about?
choices: Learning how light helps plants grow
questions.rationale: The whole passage follows one test and what the group learned from it.

questions.prompt: Where did Mina put her cup?
questions.rationale: The second sentence directly tells which tool or place was used.

questions.prompt: What did the group check or write down?
questions.rationale: The fourth sentence explains what the group checked or recorded.

questions.prompt: What does difference mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: A loud sound in a room
The way two things are not the same
questions.rationale: Difference means the two results did not match each other.

questions.prompt: What does stem mean in the fourth sentence?
choices: A person who visits a club
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from how it is used in the passage.

questions.prompt: Why did Joon move his cup closer to the window?
choices: He thought more light would help his plant
The teacher told the student to stop working
The group wanted to decorate the room more
The first tool was missing from the class
questions.rationale: The student changed after seeing which method worked better.
